exemplar.title: To what extent does oxytocin levels influence interpersonal trust in young adults?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does oxytocin levels influence interpersonal trust in young adults?

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Consistent, accurate communication of topic and clear, focused research question
Wellâdefined methodology with appropriate selection and justified use of primary studies
Logical outline linking all sections back to the guiding research purpose
weaknesses: Occasional repetition in the explanation of the topic
Minor methodological transparency gaps (e.g., missing database names and search keywords)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three Focus and Method strands meet the topâband descriptors: the topic and purpose are communicated effectively; the research question is clearly stated, focused and drives the discussion; and the methodology is comprehensive, with clear evidence of informed source selection. The minor lapses (some repetitiveness, implicit rather than explicit description of database searches) do not outweigh the overall strength, justifying the full 6 marks.

strengths: Sound knowledge of biological mechanisms underlying oxytocin and trust
Generally accurate application of empirical findings to support key points
Frequent correct use of subjectâspecific terminology
weaknesses: Occasional conceptual inaccuracies (e.g., conflating anxiety reduction with affiliation)
Inconsistent citation and reference formatting, and some reliance on secondary websites
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates coherent understanding of psychological concepts and applies relevant sources effectively, placing performance solidly in the 3â4 band. However, sporadic inaccuracies, uneven source use and formatting issues prevent elevation to the top band, so 4 marks are appropriate.

strengths: Wide, balanced research base including contradictory findings
Effective critical evaluation of methodological strengths and limitations
Reasoned, nuanced argument structure with a qualified conclusion
weaknesses: Analysis remains descriptive in parts without deeper integrative frameworks (e.g., missing effectâsize comparisons)
Evaluation is episodic rather than fully integrative, with limited futureâ research suggestions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research selection is consistently relevant and critically appraised, meriting topâband consideration. While analysis and evaluation occasionally lack the depth and integration of the very highest band, the overall quality of argumentation, balance of evidence and methodological critique justify a high mark of 11 out of 12.

strengths: Clear, convention-aligned structure with functional headings and pagination
Logical flow of sections that aids reader navigation
weaknesses: Visible formatting artefacts (stray symbols, page-break issues)
Inconsistent reference and citation style, occasional layout inconsistencies
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay meets all core structural conventions and overall presentation is coherent, placing it within the 3â4 band. However, recurring minor formatting and citation inconsistencies prevent a top-band score, warranting 3 out of 4 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning:
Psychology-specific terminology such as âdouble-blind,â âplacebo,â âaffiliative motivation,â âsocial salience,â âecological validity,â and âmeta-analysisâ is used frequently and usually correctly, demonstrating a solid grasp of conceptual language.

However, there are repeated minor lapses (e.g., âEnvelope Task Paradiam,â âoxytocin increase trust,â inconsistent use of âinterpersonal trustâ vs. âsocial trustâ), and reference formatting shows conceptual labels embedded in citation numbers, which reduces clarity.  A truly consistent and polished use of terminology is therefore not achieved.

Given mostly accurate yet imperfect execution, the work aligns with the 3â4 band; a top-band (5â6) score is not justified.  Four marks are therefore appropriate.

Basic layout requirements are addressed: word count, title, table of contents and page numbers appear, and in-text citations are attempted.  However, reference formatting is inconsistent (mixed APA and numeric superscripts, duplicated sources), some LaTeX control characters remain visible, and tables are sparsely populated.

These shortcomings indicate that some important layout considerations have not been correctly applied, especially in the reference section and citation style, so the work best fits the 1â2 band rather than the higher band.

Given that mostâbut not allâlayout conventions are met, the upper score of 2 within the 1â2 band is awarded.

The student shows sound knowledge of the biological basis of behaviour, clearly describing oxytocin synthesis, amygdala involvement, double-blind procedures and trust paradigms.  Summaries of Kosfeld, Baumgartner, Declerck and others are generally accurate and relevant, illustrating an understanding of how hormonal mechanisms are investigated.

Source application is mostly appropriate: empirical findings are quoted to support each point and contrasted with contradictory evidence.  Nevertheless, occasional inaccuracies (e.g., conflating anxiety reduction with affiliation in the Baumgartner analysis) and superficial use of secondary websites dilute the strength of application.

Because understanding is coherent but not uniformly âexcellentâ and a few citations are incomplete or mis-formatted, the strand best fits the upper end of the 3â4 band; thus 4 marks are awarded.

The student cites a wide spectrum of primary literature (experimental, fMRI, meta-analytic, correlational) directly addressing the oxytocinâtrust relationship; most studies are recent and peer-reviewed, matching the stated criteria.  Each sourceâs relevance to the RQ is articulated, and counter-evidence (Shou et al.; Nave et al.) is purposefully included, reflecting balanced coverage.

Less relevant popular articles (e.g., Verywell Mind) appear but are marginal, and some key landmark replication failures beyond 2015 are absent.  Overall, the selection is more than âadequateâ but not yet exhaustive or fully systematic.

Consequently, the strand meets the âgood/mostly relevantâ description within the 7â9 band, receiving the lower boundary mark of 7.

The research question â âTo what extent does oxytocin influence interpersonal trust in young adults?â â is explicitly presented beneath the title and repeated verbatim in the introduction and conclusion.  It is sufficiently narrow (specific hormone, single behaviour, defined age range) to be investigated within 4 000 words and clearly drives selection of literature.

Throughout the body of the essay the student consistently links each empirical study, criticism and synthesis back to this question, demonstrating effective connection between the RQ and ensuing discussion.  The wording of the RQ invites evaluation (âto what extentâ) and the essay does attempt a balanced judgement.

The focus could be refined further by specifying the route of administration (endogenous vs. intranasal) or by limiting the setting (economic-game paradigms versus everyday contexts).  Because that extra precision is absent, the strand earns a solid 5 rather than the full 6.

The essay follows standard EE conventions (title page with word count, table of contents, separate reference list) and sections flow logically.  Headings, sub-headings and pagination (numbers appear in the margin) enable navigation, meeting the descriptor for âclearly appropriate structure.â

Minor issues persist: occasional page-break artefacts, stray LaTeX symbols, and an abbreviated literature review header could distract the reader.  Nonetheless, all required structural elements are present and functional.

As these weaknesses are minor, the work remains within the 3â4 band.  The lower mark of 3 reflects the small but recurring formatting artefacts.

The essay presents a reasoned argument: it first outlines evidence in favour of oxytocinâs influence, then counters with non-supportive findings, and finally offers a nuanced conclusion that trust is multi-determinant.  Evaluation of individual studies (design, sample, bias) is present and mostly appropriate, showing an emerging critical stance.

However, the evaluation is episodic rather than integrative; there is limited discussion of theoretical implications (e.g., interaction between biological and cognitive levels of analysis) and no explicit consideration of future research directions.  Argument structure is clear but not sophisticated.

Consequently, the strand sits at the upper edge of the 4â6 band â a structured argument with partial critical evaluation â and earns 6 marks.

For each study the student provides concise methodological summaries followed by strengths and limitations (e.g., ecological validity, sample bias, practice effects).  Links to Bartz et al.âs three-mechanism model reveal attempts at conceptual synthesis.  The discussion weighs supporting versus refuting evidence and ends with a tentative, qualified conclusion.

Nevertheless, analysis remains descriptive in places, and logical connections are sometimes asserted rather than demonstrated (e.g., assuming generosity is a confound without exploring alternative statistical controls).  No quantitative effect-size comparison or overarching analytical framework (such as a table or meta-analytic calculation) is offered.

Thus the work surpasses âpartially relevant analysisâ and fits the top of the 4â6 band, warranting 6 marks rather than entry into the higher 7â9 band.

annotations.comment: The opening paragraph defines hormones broadly but lacks direct connection to interpersonal trust. Focus more immediately on oxytocinâs role in trust.
The detailed chemical description of oxytocin synthesis is accurate and shows solid subject knowledge of biological mechanisms.
The page number artifact at the top distracts readers. Remove manual page markers that conflict with automated pagination.
Reiterating the research question in the introduction helps readers follow the investigationâs purpose and maintains strong focus.
The inclusion/exclusion criteria are described clearly, but the database names and search keywords remain implicit. Specify these to strengthen methodology transparency.
The âReview of Literatureâ heading is formatted consistently and clearly, helping to structure the essay appropriately.
Footnotes display raw HTML entities (e.g., &lt;sup>). Convert these to proper formatting to improve readability.
The title clearly states the topic of the investigation and aligns well with the research question, providing a strong focus from the outset.
Splitting the research question across two separate headings disrupts readability. Combine the question into a single heading or line for clarity.
Including the word count at the start demonstrates compliance with formatting requirements and aids assessment.
The table of contents structure is clear, but page numbers are missing. Add accurate page references for each section.
A stray inline math expression is present in the header. Remove or convert it to regular text to avoid formatting artifacts.
The placement of the "Table of Contents" heading is well centered and visually prominent, aiding navigation.
When summarizing Kosfeld et al., integrate in-text citations in authorâdate format to align with APA conventions and avoid numeric superscripts.
Include effect-size data or specific statistical values when reporting Trust Game results to deepen analytical insight.
The possessive phrasing around Kosfeld et al. is awkward and citation punctuation is inconsistent. Edit for grammatical precision.
The critique of the single-blind design and small sample size is insightful, showing effective evaluation of methodological strengths and weaknesses.
Convert the âBio psychoâ journal title to its correct format (e.g., Biological Psychology) to maintain accuracy.
The link between amygdala deactivation findings and trust outcomes is concise but would benefit from deeper linkage back to the research question.
The discussion of sample size, practice effects, and gender bias in Baumgartner et al. demonstrates strong critical evaluation.
The discussion section effectively synthesizes supporting and conflicting findings, but consider adding an integrative theoretical framework.
When outlining Declerck et al.âs methodology, specify how randomization and control conditions were implemented for greater methodological transparency.
The endogenous measurement approach in Shou et al. adds ecological validity, illustrating careful selection of methodology.
The tense shifts between past and present tense in describing the sample and measures. Use a consistent past-tense narrative for completed studies.
When critiquing the meta-analysis, include specific effect sizes or confidence intervals to support claims of non-significance.
The quasi-experimental design critique accurately notes limitations due to confounds; consider suggesting statistical controls the original authors could apply.
Convert HTML entities in the footnote to standard superscript formatting to maintain a polished reference list.
Acknowledging that some participants knew each other in Berg et al. rightly shows attention to potential confounds and boosts the critique's depth.
The conclusion of the Berg et al. section hints at future directions but stops short. Explicitly recommend follow-up studies or unanswered questions.
The phrase âdevelopmental developmentâ is redundant and impacts readability. Replace with concise terminology.
This paragraph is overly long and mixes multiple critiques. Break into shorter paragraphs for clarity and easier reader engagement.
The Nave et al. meta-analysis footnote is misformatted, mixing authorâdate with numeric style. Standardize to APA or chosen style.
Linking neuroimaging results directly to conceptual models demonstrates strong use of biological terminology and coherent understanding.
Referencing Mikolajczak et al. without the publication year disrupts clarity. Always include authorâdate citations when introducing studies.
The conclusion restates earlier points but is repetitive. Tighten it by highlighting just two or three key takeaways and future research needs.
The reference list contains multiple citation styles and broken items. Standardize formatting (APA/Harvard) and correct incomplete entries.
